title: Full Roster Revealed for DC's "DC KO" Tournament
bodyHtml:
Building on the blockbuster announcement of its DC KO event last July, DC Comics has now unveiled the complete 32-fighter roster set to compete for the title of King Omega.

The reveal gives fans their first look at the diverse lineup of heroes and villains who will be forced to battle in a universe-altering tournament.

The lineup features a stunning mix of DC's heaviest hitters, cunning strategists, and chaotic wild cards, setting the stage for unpredictable and brutal matchups. The combatants are:

* Powergirl

* Superman

* Lex Luthor

* Supergirl

* Damian Wayne

* Batman

* Jason Todd

* Batwoman

* Plastic Man

* Aquaman

* Harley Quinn

* Lobo

* Metamorpho

* Wonder Woman

* Swamp Thing

* Firestorm

* Black Lightning

* Guy Gardner

* Giganta

* Captain Atom

* Jay Garrick

* Cheetah

* Cyborg

* Big Barda

* Connor Hawke

* Star Sapphire

* Vixen

* Zatanna

* King Shark

* Hawkman

* Etrigan

* Starro

The reveal of who is fighting adds a new layer of gravity to the stakes of the tournament. The single surviving champion will be imbued with enough Omega energy to fight a fused Darkseid/Spectre entity. As event architect Scott Snyder warned during the initial announcement, “You have to be merciless to win. And when you’re out, you’re out.”

Co-architect Joshua Williamson’s previous comments also take on new meaning now that the combatants are known. “Each level is a new challenge," Williamson stated. "Readers are going to see unexpected fighters rise in ways they won’t see coming."

With the fighters now confirmed, DC has announced it will roll out official bracket breakdowns, fighter spotlights, and previews of the opening round matchups in the weeks ahead, fueling speculation on who might ultimately claim the crown.

heroImage.alt: Full Roster Revealed for DC's "DC KO" Tournament

title: Don't Bring A Robin to a KnightFight: Everything We Know About DC's Epic Batman Event!
bodyHtml:
Get ready to witness the ultimate showdown in Gotham! DC Comics is set to unleash Knightfight, a new Batman-centric event that promises to be a thrilling, action-packed ride. Tying directly into the massive DC K.O. crossover, Knightfight pits the Dark Knight against the most challenging opponents he could ever face: his own sidekicks!

Batman's Most Personal Battle Yet

In a recent interview, legendary writer Scott Snyder pulled back the curtain on this highly anticipated series, revealing it is a deeply personal journey for the Caped Crusader. Knightfight features Batman battling alternate versions of his former Robins who have grown up to become their own versions of the Caped Crusader. Snyder describes it as "Batman's journey through Hell."

This epic event is spearheaded by the dynamic team of writer Joshua Williamson with a little help from Scott Snyder and the phenomenal art of Dan Mora. The creative powerhouse is set to deliver "pure comic book joy with a lot of heart," according to Snyder.

A Tie-In to DC's "King Omega" Event

Knightfight is part of the larger DC K.O. event, where heroes are forced to compete in a cosmic tournament. The event's title, which stands for King Omega, refers to an omnipotent Darkseid who has become the "totality of Omega energy." The tournament is meant to find a champion to face him. As part of this, DC has announced "Fight Month" in December, featuring various character matches, including one written by Snyder himself!

Snyder also revealed a deeper, more personal meaning to the larger story. While Knightfight focuses on Batman's emotional gauntlet, the overarching DC K.O. event is a personal Superman story, exploring what Snyder thinks makes the Man of Steel a truly great character.

Get ready for an explosive November when Knightfight hits comic book stores, and prepare for a series that is set to be one of the most exciting Batman stories in years!

(Source: Scott Snyder's recent interview as reported by Bleeding Cool and other news outlets.)

Are you ready for Batman to face his Robins? Who do you think will be the toughest challenge for the Dark Knight? Share your thoughts and predictions in the comments below!

heroImage.alt: Don't Bring A Robin to a KnightFight: Everything We Know About DC's Epic Batman Event!

title: Get Ready for a Cosmic Collision: A Deep Dive into DC's "King Omega" Event!
bodyHtml:
Hold onto your capes, DC fans! The next major comic book event is not what you think it is, and the details are even more epic than we imagined.

In an interview that has the entire industry buzzing, legendary writer Scott Snyder himself revealed the truth behind the title DC K.O. As reported by our friends at Bleeding Cool, the "K.O." does not stand for "Knockout" but for the terrifying new threat: King Omega.

A Multiverse-Spanning Tournament of Power!

This is a cosmic, gladiatorial free-for-all on an unprecedented scale! DC’s greatest heroes are being thrown into a massive tournament with one desperate goal: to create a single, all-powerful champion. Their mission is to generate enough raw Omega energy to stand against the ultimate enemy—a terrifyingly omnipotent Darkseid who has ascended to the title of "King Omega" in the Absolute Universe!

Scott Snyder revealed that the tournament will feature a staggering 36 fighters from across the DC Universe, all duking it out on Earth, which has been transformed into a five-level gladiatorial arena. This is about a desperate, noble cause to create a hero strong enough to challenge a being who is the "entire totality of Omega energy."

A Fresh and Personal Story with Far-Reaching Consequences!

This crossover promises to be a deeply personal and groundbreaking story. Snyder noted that unlike Marvel, DC has not done many large-scale "fight events," making this a truly unique and fresh take on a classic comic trope. The heart of the story, as Snyder explains, is a very personal one wrapped in "nine layers of candy exploding in your face."

The action will not be contained to the main five-issue series, either! This event is set to have major tie-ins with your favorite titles, including Justice League: The Omega Act, Titans, Superman, Justice League Unlimited, and The Flash. Prepare for a wave of new "Absolute Universe" comics and major line changes spinning out of this event.

This is a cosmic showdown to save the entire universe from an all-powerful Darkseid, and it’s an event you absolutely will not want to miss!

What do you think of a DC Comics gladiator tournament? Will your favorite hero be the one to become the ultimate champion? Let us know in the comments!

(Source: Bleeding Cool's report on Scott Snyder's interview)

heroImage.alt: Get Ready for a Cosmic Collision: A Deep Dive into DC's "King Omega" Event!

title: Marvel Jumping on Grab Bag Trend with Ultimate Endgame #1
bodyHtml:
Following the success of Image Comics and Skybound Entertainment's blind bag release for Invincible Universe: Battle Beast, which garnered over 400,000 orders, Marvel Comics is now joining the trend.

The publisher is launching its own blind bag program for the upcoming Ultimate Endgame #1, a five-issue series written by Deniz Camp with art by Jonas Scharf and Terry Dodson. This initiative is designed to emulate the excitement of opening a pack of trading cards in hopes of finding a rare item.

The "True Believers Blind Bags" will be sealed, and each will contain one of the many available covers for the issue. Randomly inserted packs will also include variant covers exclusive to the blind bag program, including "secret" covers that will remain unrevealed until a collector opens the bag.

This new program provides a fresh way for local comic shops to engage with their customers and offers collectors an exciting opportunity to hunt for rare variants.

heroImage.alt: Marvel Jumping on Grab Bag Trend with Ultimate Endgame #1

In mind, body, and spirit, Batman has trained himself to the peak of human ability. But even the World’s Greatest Detective has his limits—and now he has an enemy who knows exactly where they are.

Known as the Black Glove, this mysterious adversary intends to crush the Dark Knight completely, using the one weapon against which he has no defense: his own mind.

As Batman struggles to hold on to his sanity, an alliance of criminal masterminds is free to make its move on Gotham City. To survive, the Dark Knight must go deeper within himself than ever before—but once the truth behind the Black Glove is revealed, will the Batman ever be able to find his way back out?

GREEN LANTERN BY ROBERT VENDITTI

Highfather of the New Gods has become convinced that the Life Equation can be accessed by combining the colors of the emotional spectrum through the rings of the universe’s Lantern Corps. Believing the ring bearers are too unevolved to wield power that they do not fully understand, Highfather declares war on all who wield a power ring!

But what happens when the Green Lantern Corps goes missing, and Hal is the universe’s lone renegade

Green Lantern with one of the most powerful weapons in the universe?

Green Lantern by Robert Venditti Omnibus Vol. 2 collects Green Lantern #35-52, Green Lantern Annual #3-4, Green Lantern: Futures End #1, Green Lantern/New Gods: Godhead #1, Green Lantern Corps #35-40, Green Lantern Corps: Futures End #1, Green Lantern: New Guardians #35-37, Red Lanterns #35-37, and Sinestro #6-8.

JUSTICE LEAGUE OF AMERICA: THE NAIL: THE COMPLETE DELUXE EDITION (2026 EDITION)

Written by ALAN DAVIS

Art and Cover by ALAN DAVIS and MARK FARMER

$49.99 US | 312 pages | 7 1/16″ x 10 7/8″| Hardcover | ISBN: 978-1-79950-661-4

ON SALE 1/13/26

Formed by the World’s Greatest Heroes, the Justice League of America stands tall as the mightiest group of protectors Earth has to offer. Since the group’s founding, one hero above all others has stood as their inspiration, guiding light, and moral center: Superman. Now imagine a world without the Man of Steel.

Formed by the World’s Greatest Heroes, the Justice League of America stands tall as the mightiest group of protectors Earth has to offer. Since the group’s founding, one hero above all others has stood as their inspiration, guiding light, and moral center: Superman. Now imagine a world without the Man of Steel.

Welcome to the alternate history of JLA: The Nail.

In a fascinating story of tiny accidents that cascade into cataclysmic disasters, writer/artist Alan Davis (Batman and the Outsiders) joined by legendary inker Mark Farmer (Legion of Super-Heroes) creates a world that never knew a Superman and a Justice League that is about to face their greatest test of might and morality. All because of something as simple as a nail.

SUPERMAN: NEW KRYPTON SAGA

Art by PETE WOODS, RENATO GUEDES, JAMAL IGLE, and more

Superman was not Krypton’s only survivor…

The great city of Kandor was home to Krypton’s best and brightest before Brainiac used his terrifying technology to shrink and abduct the entire city. Thanks to Superman, Kandor has been freed and returned to its normal size. And it has a new home planet: Earth. Now tens of thousands of Kryptonians, each with the godlike powers of Superman himself, are roaming free. Will they usher in a new golden age for their human neighbors, or will they choose to rule instead? Collecting in reading orderr for the first time ever Action Comics #866-879, Adventure Comics Special Featuring the Guardian #1, Supergirl #35-42, Superman #681-685, Superman’s Pal, Jimmy Olsen Special #1, Superman: World of New Krypton #1-3, Superman: World of New Krypton Special #1, and stories from Action Comics Annual #10 and Supergirl Annual #1

Across thousands of worlds, the dead have risen. These Black Lanterns, heroes and villains recruited from beyond the grave, have one purpose: to extinguish all light—and life—in the universe. Hal Jordan has overcome death once before. As the greatest Green Lantern to ever wield the emerald ring, it is Hal’s unquestioned duty to lead the living against this overwhelming army of the dead. While Blackest Night consumes Earth’s heroes, the seven Corps, empowered by the emotional spectrum, must end their war against each other—or bear witness to the end of all creation. As Earth becomes the ultimate battleground between the living and the dead, there is only one certainty: no one escapes death.

Superstar writer Geoff Johns (Green Lantern, Justice League, Batman: Earth One) and artists Ivan Reis (Aquaman) and Doug Mahnke (Green Lantern) exhume the DC Universe’s greatest secrets in this emotional epic that grabs its heroes by their hearts. This comprehensive Absolute Edition collects Blackest Night #0-8 and Green Lantern #43-48 and #50-52 as well as deleted scenes, the complete script to Blackest Night #1, series commentary from writer Geoff Johns, and more than 30 pages of sketch material.

title: Valiant Responds to Accusations of Transphobia in Bloodshot
bodyHtml:
In response to accusations of transphobia, Alien Books, the publisher for Valiant Comics, alongside writer Mauro Mantella, has issued a sincere apology for any harm caused by dialogue in Bloodshot #1. The controversy stemmed from a specific panel where a line of dialogue, set within a fictional world of vampires and cults, was interpreted as an allusion to real-world discrimination against transgender people. This interpretation sparked significant backlash on social media platforms like Reddit, with prominent comic creators such as former Bloodshot writer Deniz Camp and creator Zoe Tunnell publicly condemning the panel. Camp asserted that Bloodshot would be a firm ally of the trans community, while Tunnell expressed her disgust and refused to work with the publisher while those responsible were still employed.

That transphobic bullshit in the pages of the new Bloodshot cannot be tolerated.

— Zac Thompson (@zacthompson.bsky.social) August 15, 2025 at 11:53 AM

I pride myself on my professionalism in comics. I don't talk shit about books I don't enjoy. I am happy when folks get gigs even if I wish I landed them. So when I say the transphobic bullshit in the new BLOODSHOT #1 comic is disgusting and should have never made it to print. Shameful shit.

[image or embed]

— Zoe Tunnell @ FlameCon Saturday (@zoewithasword.bsky.social) August 15, 2025 at 12:05 PM

Bloodshot would hate transphobes and throw himself in front of a hail of bullets to protect a trans kid without hesitation. Just FYI.

— Deniz Camp (@DenizCamp) August 15, 2025

The publisher clarified that this was never their intention, attributing the issue to a nuance lost in translation, as the original line was written by an Argentinian creator. Acknowledging that their intent did not negate the harmful impact, Valiant has committed to updating the dialogue in all digital and future collected editions of the comic. Moving forward, they have pledged to implement a more intensive review process for all scripts, involving closer scrutiny by proofreaders to ensure clearer and more responsible storytelling. The publisher also expressed gratitude for the community's feedback, reaffirming their commitment to empathy and inclusion in their work.

Valiant Comics, via its publisher Alien Books, has issued the following statement, saying it is all something that got lost in translation:

"Alien Books and writer Mauro Mantella sincerely apologize for the harm caused by the phrasing in Bloodshot #1. While the story takes place in a fictional world of vampires and cults, we understand that a specific line of dialogue has been read as alluding to real-world issues, specifically, the discrimination faced by trans people. That was never the intention. The original line was written by an Argentinian creator and was unfortunately a case of nuance being lost in translation. We fully recognize that intent does not erase impact, especially when dealing with subject matter that affects real lives and communities. We are taking the following steps immediately:

  • The dialogue in question will be updated for all digital and collected editions to better reflect the intended fictional context.

  • Going forward, all scripts will undergo a more intense review by our proof readers as part of our editorial process to ensure clearer, more responsible storytelling.

  • We appreciate the feedback from readers, creators, and industry peers who brought this to our attention. We take this seriously and are committed to learning from it.

  • Alien Books values inclusion, empathy, and creative responsibility. We will do better."

Vampires in fiction are usually used as analogies to modern-day concerns, and it's quite natural to look for parallels in vampire fiction. In this case, Valiant is saying it is about cults. Complete and utter ones.

heroImage.alt: Valiant Responds to Accusations of Transphobia in Bloodshot

title: Infernal Hulk: The Hulk You Always Feared Is Here
bodyHtml:
Get ready, Marvel fans, because the Incredible Hulk is about to get a whole lot more... well, infernal. Writers Phillip Kennedy Johnson and artist Nic Klein—the creative minds behind the recent horror-fueled Incredible Hulk run—are back this November with Infernal Hulk #1, and it’s introducing the most terrifying version of the character ever.

​This isn't just another super-powered rampage. The series is a direct result of the events in Incredible Hulk #30, where Hulk faced off against the cosmic-level horror known as the Eldest. Now, the Eldest has taken control of Hulk’s body, tearing him and Bruce Banner apart to usher in a new "Age of Monsters" for the entire Marvel Universe.

​A New Age of Monsters

​The new Infernal Hulk is more than just strong; it's a diabolical, ancient force bent on transforming the heroes you know and love into monstrous beasts. The Eldest's plan is to plunge the world back into a nightmarish, primeval state.

​But don't count Bruce Banner out just yet. Though powerless, he's determined to stop this new, unstoppable Hulk from the inside. It's a fight for the soul of the Marvel Universe, and it's unlike anything we've seen before.

​What the Creators Have to Say

​According to Phillip Kennedy Johnson, this new series is a "terrifying new normal" for the Hulk and the entire Marvel Universe. He promises fans will get all the answers they've been waiting for regarding the Eldest and the Mother of Horrors. Johnson teases that there are even more powerful monsters coming, with origins that predate humanity. "No corner of the Marvel Universe will be left untouched before the end," he says.

​Artist Nic Klein shares that working with Johnson has been a dream, calling Infernal Hulk the natural destination for their journey with the character. He’s pouring everything he has into the book and is excited for fans to see it.

​Infernal Hulk #1 is written by Phillip Kennedy Johnson and features art and a cover by Nic Klein. It's scheduled to hit comic shops on November 26. Make sure to check out the chilling cover and preorder your copy today!

heroImage.alt: Infernal Hulk: The Hulk You Always Feared Is Here

title: Deadpool/Batman Crossover from DC Comics Reveals Bonus Stories
bodyHtml:
Coming out on November 19, 2025, the 64-page crossover comic Batman/Deadpool #1 unites Batman and Deadpool in a cosmic, metafictional adventure.

The main story, written by Grant Morrison and illustrated by Dan Mora, blends the Dark Knight's brooding nature with the Merc with a Mouth's fourth-wall-breaking chaos. Described as "200% more Grant Morrison-esque than ever before," this one-shot sends the characters on a journey across different realities, from Apokolips to Earth-TRN666, with the very fabric of reality at risk.

The comic also includes four bonus team-up stories from other top creators:

  • Scott Snyder, James Tynion IV, Joshua Williamson, and Hayden Sherman pair Constantine with Doctor Strange.

  • Tom Taylor and Bruno Redondo bring Nightwing (Dick Grayson) and Wolverine (Laura Kinney) together.

  • Mariko Tamaki and Amanda Conner feature a comedic fight between Harley Quinn and Hulk.

  • G. Willow Wilson and Denys Cowan team up Static and Ms. Marvel.

This issue follows Marvel’s own Deadpool/Batman #1, which is set to be released on September 17.

To make this event even bigger, Batman/Deadpool #1 will have an extensive lineup of variant covers from some of the biggest names in comics, with homages to classic covers like Crisis on Infinite Earths #7 and The Incredible Hulk #340, and other pairings like Wonder Woman and Scarlet Witch, and Lobo and Deadpool.

Check some of them out below

title: DC Comics Announces 'Batman/Static: Beyond' 6-Part Elseworlds Series
bodyHtml:
The rumors are true! DC Comics has officially announced a new six-part Elseworlds miniseries titled Batman/Static: Beyond. This highly anticipated comic brings together the fan-favorite heroes Terry McGinnis (Batman Beyond) and Virgil Hawkins (Static Shock) in a new, standalone adventure.

What to Expect:

  • A New Elseworlds Tale: Set outside of current DC continuity, the story finds Terry McGinnis defending Neo-Gotham while Virgil Hawkins is a technological pioneer. A mysterious global blackout forces these two iconic heroes to join forces.

  • The Creative Team: The series is helmed by writer Evan Narcisse (Rise of the Black Panther) and artist Nikolas Draper-Ivey, who previously worked together on the celebrated Static: Shadows of Dakota series.

  • Release Date: The first issue, Batman/Static: Beyond #1, is scheduled to hit comic book stores and digital platforms on November 12, 2025.

“This story is intended to be a commentary on our overreliance on technology and the imminent dangers becoming complacent and too comfortable using AI to substitute our own imagination and human ingenuity,” continued Nikolas Draper-Ivey. “We now use our watches to find our phones, we use our phones to connect to everything else. We don’t rely on our own memories. We rely on technology to remember for us. That deeply concerns me. I worry that we are getting to a point where if all these things were to be suddenly taken away from us in an instant—at the rate we’re going now, humanity will be at a huge disadvantage.”

This project marks a significant new chapter for both characters, offering fans a fresh take on their dynamic and an exciting exploration of their shared future.

heroImage.alt: DC Comics Announces 'Batman/Static: Beyond' 6-Part Elseworlds Series

title: BREAKING: DC's "Power Company" is BACK!
bodyHtml:
This April marks the return of Josiah Power and a new iteration of The Power Company to DC All In with The Power Company: Recharged, and DC is revealing a first look at interior art from issue #1 by Khary Randolph and Alitha Martinez. This DC All In one-shot not only features the return of writer Hill to DC and the return of Josiah Power but brings together a collection of DC Super Heroes – Batman (Jace Fox), Vixen, and Duke Thomas, aka The Signal – to join Black Lightning in fighting anti-metahuman sentiment at the street level (thanks to Amanda Waller and Absolute Power).

Spinning out of the events of Black Lightning and DC Power: Rise of the Power Company, Josiah Power and Jefferson Pierce lead the newest iteration of the Power Company with a new purpose of rebuilding humanity’s faith in heroes and fighting back against the rising anti-metahuman movement that Amanda Waller instigated in DC’s Absolute Power.

Bryan Edward Hill makes his return to DC comics. He recently revitalized Blade for Marvel and is doing a stellar job on Ultimate Black Panther. He brings his talent back to DC to raise the bar for these great heroes. Bryan did a fantastic job with Batman and the Outsiders and now he's back to tackle more characters on the edge of greatness. DC has been ALL IN on many of its heroes in this current era and we cannot wait to see how he elevates these heroes to a new level of prominence and recognition.

For their first mission the Company dispatches Batman (Jace Fox), The Signal, and Vixen to investigate a serial killer that’s been hunting metahumans in the streets of Atlanta.

Variant Covers

Check out some of the variant covers. One of which has already become a popular meme template.

heroImage.alt: BREAKING: DC's "Power Company" is BACK!

title: BREAKING: DC Comics Announces New Superman ANTHOLOGY Series
bodyHtml:
Superman: The World: a Celebrated Lineup of International Storytellers Pay Tribute to DC’s Man of Steel in a Commemorative Hardcover Collection

Burbank, CA, (March 31, 2025) – Since his origin in 1938, Superman has become a symbol of truth and justice, and a true worldwide pop culture icon. Today DC announced talent and story details on Superman: The World, a 208-page hardcover collecting original stories celebrating Superman from a variety of international and cultural perspectives. Superman: The World arrives in participating comic book shops, booksellers, and mass market retailers and e-tailers Wednesday, June 25, and launches day and date in the following countries: Argentina (Ovni Press); Bosnia, Montenegro, and Serbia (Carobna); Brazil (Panini); Cameroon (Zebra Comics); the Czech and Slovak Republics (Crew); France (Urban Comics); Germany (Panini); India (DC/Penguin Random House); Italy (Panini); Japan (Kodansha); Mexico (Panini); Poland (Story House Egmont); Spain (Panini); and Turkey (JBC).

“Let Slip the Dogs of War,” by Dan Jurgens and Lee Weeks (North America)

When a giant alien of unknown origin attacks Metropolis, the immediate response is a massive military strike that would eradicate the threat as well as the city itself. Dan Jurgens (Action Comics, Superman) and Lee Weeks (Superman: Lois and Clark, Batman/Catwoman) deliver a story in which Superman is challenged by an extraterrestrial threat and his own government’s worst instincts. It’s a singularly unique challenge for the Man of Steel, who finds himself trapped between two tough choices with little time to find a solution.

 

“The Last Seed of Krypton,” by Mauro Mantella and Agustín Alessio (Argentina)

Writer Mantella and artist Alessio book a trip for the Man of Steel to Argentina, where, in search of a story as Clark Kent and a potential threat as Superman, he encounters an ancient menace with ties to his past—one that threatens the entire planet and forces him to reconsider his very essence and biology.

“The Red Mantle,” by Jefferson Costa (Brazil)

In this story by writer/artist Costa, Clark Kent is in Brazil conducting a series of interviews following the opening of an exhibition at the national museum marking the return and restitution of the Tupinambá red cape, repatriated to Brazilian territory after 400 years. Clark is intrigued and enthusiastic about the history of the artifact, which holds such strong and significant symbolism, representing the cultural revival of a people, memory, and ancestry—a symbolism very similar to his own red cape. When the Tupinambá cloak disappears from the museum, Superman goes into action, determined to recover it.

“Chariot of the Gods,” by Dr. Ejob Gaius, E.N. Ejob, and Coeurtys Minko (Cameroon)

Following their groundbreaking story in Joker: The World, the Zebra Comics team of writer Gaius and artists Ejob  and Minko tell their own unique story spotlighting the Man of Steel. Superman faces an unusual culture shock as his noble intentions lead to unforeseen consequences. Through this eye-opening journey, he discovers the profound importance of understanding and respecting cultural differences, adding to what it truly means to be a hero for the world.

“If Nihilism Is the Answer, What Was the Question?,” by Štěpán Kopřiva and Michal Suchánek  (Czech and Slovak Republics)

Superman has always been a symbol of hope, but what happens when he encounters a community that doesn’t believe in hope? The Czech storytelling team of writer Kopřiva and artist Suchánek put this question to the Man of Steel when, in the distant future, he encounters “Prague,” a Czech space station with a single occupant, one who believes in absolutely nothing—order, values, hope—nor in the meaning of it all.

“Superman in Paris,” by Sylvain Runberg, Marcial Toledano Vargas, and José Manuel Robledo (France)

This short story by writer Runberg (The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo/Millenium, Warship Jolly Roger, Under York) and artists Toledano Vargas (Ken Games, Dominants) and Robledo (Tebori) tells the tale of how Lois Lane and Clark Kent hope to escape their daily adventures for a brief getaway to Paris, the City of Lights. But there’s something lurking in the depths of the Seine River that may interrupt their romantic interlude.

“Man of Kruppstahl,” by Flix (Felix Görmann) (Germany)

In his unique style, award-winning writer/artist Flix (Spirou, The Humboldt Animal) tells a nostalgic and charming story from Superman’s past in which the Man of Steel thwarts the machinations of a villain in postwar Germany.

“To Be a Hero,” by Rana Daggubati and Sid Kotian (India)

In this story by Daggubati and Kotian, Clark Kent is covering a story in India and meets a woman studying history. They’re approached by a young boy, looking for help. It’s up to Superman to keep bandits from pillaging his local temple of its statues and artifacts.

“Superman’s Inferno,” by Fabio Celoni and Marco Nucci (Italy)

Acclaimed Italian comics creators Nucci and Celoni celebrate the incredible narrative of the Last Son of Krypton in this short story. In 2021, Clark Kent and Lois Lane are in the home city of the Sommo Poeta (the Supreme Poet), Dante Alighieri, on the 700th anniversary of his death. Suddenly, the earth splits open, with Lois and some bystanders swallowed up in a chasm that seems to lead directly to Hell. Superman’s only hope against the armies of Lucifer is tied to a formula seen in Dante’s visions that has been lost forever!

 “Superman vs. Convenience-Store Dining Space,” by Satoshi Miyagawa and Kai Kitago (Japan)

DC and Kodansha will include an excerpt from the popular DC manga Superman vs. Meshi, by Miyagawa and Kitago, readable in Western style (left to right).

“Superman: The Left of the Hummingbird,” by Bef (Bernardo Fernández) (Mexico)

When Clark Kent visits Mexico for a simple news story, Superman ends up in a battle against the Aztec gods in this story by writer/artist Bef.

“Marzanna,” by Bartosz Sztybor and Marek Oleksicki (Poland)

Writer Sztybor and artist Oleksicki bring the Man of Steel to Warsaw, where an investigative assignment takes an unexpected turn. As Clark Kent delves into the local tradition of drowning the Marzanna effigy, he uncovers a chilling mystery tied to ancient legends and a looming threat over the city. In a race against time, Superman must confront forces beyond imagination to protect the people of Warsaw and prove that hope is the greatest power of all.

“My Choice, Protecting the Light,” by Stevan Subic (Serbia)

Storyteller Subic (The Riddler: Year One, Batman: Full Moon) both writes and draws this Superman story. After a big cosmic fight, recovering under the sun, Superman hears a faint cry for help coming from a town in Serbia, the only place on Earth that has Kryptonite, whose guardians are keeping the stash secure from anyone who might want to get their hands on it. A not-fully-healed Superman faces the challenge of helping the people protecting him from the substance most deadly to him while a deadly cosmic adversary pulls out all the stops to claim the hoard of Kryptonite.

“Superman in Granada,” by Jorge Jiménez and Alejandro Sánchez (Spain)

This short story, written and illustrated by Jorge Jiménez (Batman, Superman, Justice League, Super Sons) with colors by Alejandro Sánchez (Superman, Super Sons, Justice League), tells of how, after once again saving Earth from the imminent impact of a meteorite, Superman falls powerlessly into the Alhambra in Granada, Spain. Aware that his powers could return at any moment, he chooses to wander through the city in his costume like any other human and discover the beauty and culture Granada has to offer.

“The Hero and the Bull,” by Ethem Onur Bilgiç (Turkey)

Writer/illustrator/graphic designer Ethem Onur Bilgiç takes Clark Kent and Lois Lane to Istanbul to cover an archaeological symposium. They meet a scientist who introduces them to the rituals associated with celebrating the pagan deity Mithras, leading Clark and Lois to an unexpected encounter.

What a huge amount of international talent!

 

For the latest news on Superman: The World and all things DC, follow @DCOfficial on social media or visit https://www.dc.com. Fans can also browse a robust collection of Superman stories and experience international takes on Batman (Batman: The World) and The Joker (Joker: The World) with a subscription to the DC UNIVERSE INFINITE digital subscription platform (DCUI). For more information and a free trial, visit the DCUI website at https://www.dcuniverseinfinite.com. DCUI is not available in all countries and is not intended for children.

title: PREVIEW : Superman #25 The Return of Lex Luthor?!
bodyHtml:
On April 23, Superman reaches a major DC All In milestone with its 25th issue! This oversized issue is written by Joshua Williamson, with art by Dan Mora, Eddy Barrows and Eber Ferreira and features the DC All In debut of the real Lex Luthor! He’s got a restored memory (he lost it during the ”House of Brainiac” arc), and that means big trouble for Supercorp, Superwoman, Superman, and the city of Metropolis!

The summer of Superman is upon us and we get the return of the villainous Lex Luthor. Lex is always a game changer and now that he's back to his nefarious ways, it will definitely impact Superman and the rest of the DC universe.

Superman #25 features a wraparound main cover by series artist Mora. The main cover art is also available as a foil-embossed wraparound variant cover, with additional variant covers by Raf Grassetti, Guillem March, Jeff Dekal, Brad Walker, Dave Johnson, Laura Braga, Fico Ossio and Dan Hipp. Local comic book shops have the latest information on availability and preordering.

Check out the gorgeous covers here and a sneak peek at some of the interior action!

Variant Covers

title: BREAKING: ‘New History of the DC Universe’ by Mark Waid Announced At DC Comics!
bodyHtml:
DC, home of the World’s Greatest Super Heroes, today announced plans to publish New History of the DC Universe, a four-issue limited series arriving at participating comic book shops and digital retailers Wednesday, June 25.

DC superfan and writer Mark Waid (Batman/Superman: World’s Finest, Justice League Unlimited, Batman & Robin: Year One) teams up with some of DC’s greatest artists for this fresh look at the past and present of the DC Universe, seen through the eyes of Barry Allen, a.k.a. the Flash.

 “This is my dream project,” said series writer Waid. “It’s a chance to realign all of DC's sprawling continuity into one master timeline, and to be joined by some of comics’ greatest artists to make it shine. With new information for even longtime fans, plus Easter eggs galore, this series will be an essential read for DC fans.”

 The debut issue features art by Jerry Ordway and Todd Nauck, who join Waid in chronicling Barry Allen’s journey from the birth of the DC Universe to the rise of the Justice Society and the Golden Age of Heroes.

 “I’m having an absolute blast with this project,” said co-artist Nauck. “Mark has come up with something cool for me to draw on every page, and it’s been an honor to share art chores with a comics legend like Jerry Ordway. The DC editorial team has coordinated a wealth of deep-dive references for me to draw from, and I’m learning new aspects of DC history I hadn’t known before—and I’ve been reading DC comics for decades!”

 “As one of the artists who was part of Crisis on Infinite Earths in the 1980’s, added co-artist Jerry Ordway, I am thrilled to be involved in telling the story of this newest DC Universe with Mark and Todd."

Not only will this must-read series celebrating 90 years of DC feature an all-star lineup of interior artists, but a fan-favorite roster of cover artists will also lend their creative talents. The debut issue features a main cover from Batman & Robin: Year One artist Chris Samnee, as well as variant covers by Dan Mora (Justice League Unlimited, Superman), Ryan Sook (Black Canary: Best of the Best), and Mike Cho. Sook also provides a breathtaking wraparound foil variant, with an additional foil variant from acclaimed cover artist Stanley “Artgerm” Lau. Artist Scott Koblish will provide a series of connecting wraparound variant covers for the series, depicting DC’s iconic characters chronologically with each issue. Participating comic book shops will have details on preordering (beginning Friday, March 21).

Book One Available for Preorder Friday, March 21

On Sale at Participating Comic Book Shops

and Digital Retailers Wednesday, June 25

title: PREVIEW: Chef Guy Fieri Joins the DC Universe!
bodyHtml:
The World’s Finest are on their way to FLAVORTOWN!

On Wednesday, March 19, celebrity chef and Food Network personality Guy Fieri, aka “The Mayor of Flavortown,” becomes part of DC’s comic book universe, joining iconic DC Super Heroes Batman and Superman on a special “celebrity cameo” variant cover of DC’s Batman/Superman: World’s Finest issue #37, drawn by series artist Dan Mora.

Fieri, host of fan-favorite Food Network series such as “Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives,” “Guy’s Grocery Games” and others, joins an impressive list of celebrities and pop/geek culture icons who have been immortalized on variant covers for the series, including musicians Jack White and Sir Paul McCartney, comedian Jerry Seinfeld, and actors William Shatner and Nicolas Cage.

It's always a great thing when you see celebrities joining in on the fun of comics. The mass appeal of super heroes goes beyond the regular guy. People like Jerry Seinfeld, Shaquille O'Neal, Channing Tatum, Rosario Dawson and many more have let their love of “nerd” culture be a publicly known. Now we have Guy on the cover of a fantastic book drawn by Dan Mora. Flavortown has never looked better!

WHERE: Batman/Superman: World’s Finest #37 is available to preorder now at participating comic book shops. For the shop nearest you, visit the Comic Shop Locator website, https://www.comicshoplocator.com.

 WHEN: Batman/Superman: World’s Finest #37 is available at participating comic book shops and digital retailers on Wednesday, March 19, 2025. For more information on DC, follow @DCOfficial on social media (and @dc.com on Bluesky), or visit the official DC website at https://www.dc.com.

title: PREVIEW: DC's Absolute Green Lantern Is Set To CHANGE THE GAME !
bodyHtml:
Without the Corps…without the ring…without the willpower, what’s left is the Absolute Green Lantern!

The Absolute Universe has been burning bright, and now it's expanding even further. Acclaimed writer Al Ewing (Metamorpho: The Element Man, Immortal Hulk, Immortal Thor) joins forces with artist Jahnoy Lindsay (DC Power, Superboy: The Man of Tomorrow) to deliver Absolute Green Lantern, a bold new take on Hal Jordan, John Stewart, Jo Mullein, and more. The series launches in comic book stores on Wednesday, April 2.

With Absolute Batman, Absolute Superman, and Absolute Wonder Woman already cemented as essential reads, Absolute Green Lantern is poised to be another standout. Ewing has a proven track record of breathing new life into characters and mythologies, as seen in his transformative work on Hulk, Thor, and Venom. At MegaCon 2025, Scott Snyder shared insights into the series, teasing a fresh approach to the Emerald Space Force—one that deviates from the ever-present cosmic authority of the standard DC Universe.

Be sure to check out the preview pages—this is one book you won’t want to miss!

title: PREVIEW: DC's Peacemaker Presents The Vigilante/Eagly Double Feature!
bodyHtml:
Peacemaker fans rejoice! A comic based on and set in the Universe of DC’s Peacemaker on MAX is headed our way! What kind of hijinks will our favorite morally grey characters get in this whole new medium? Well CBC has the scoop!

DC Exclusive Preview

Peacemaker Presents: The Vigilante/Eagly Double Feature

Today, DC unveiled artwork and covers for Peacemaker Presents: The Vigilante/Eagly Double Feature! This five-issue DC Black Label limited series is inspired by DC Studios’ James Gunn’s visionary Peacemaker series on Max and features two original stories: one spotlighting Peacemaker and his avian sidekick Eagly, and one his crime-fighting friend Adrian Chase, a.k.a. Vigilante. Gunn also serves as story consultant for the series, which debuts at participating comic book shops and digital retailers March 26.

In “Peaceful Vacation,” writer Rex Ogle and artists Matteo Lolli and John Kalisz take Eagly and Chris Smith (a.k.a. Peacemaker) to the great outdoors of Alaska for some well-deserved rest and relaxation from doling out peace in their own over-the-top style. But when their trip is interrupted by a group of masked hijackers (and an annoying flight attendant), it’s up to a certain eagle and his pet human to save the day.

Unfortunately, Peacemaker forgets to tell his pal Vigilante about his vacation plans, and now Vigilante thinks Peacemaker and Eagly have been kidnapped by the Controller, which means big trouble for the criminals of Evergreen, with knives, axes, and gore galore! “A Time for Fables” is written by Tim Seeley and features art by Mitch Gerads. Each issue of the series will include diary entries from Vigilante on “How to Be A Super-Hero,” written by Freddie Stroma, who portrays the character in the Max series.

This is perfect timing for this series to come out as it was also announced that Peacemaker Season 2 would be debuting later this year on MAX. Check out this sneak peek at the story below!

For the latest news on all things DC, follow @DCOfficial on social media and visit the DC website at https://www.dc.com. title: The Justice League Continues its Epic Battle Against the Legion of Doom in DC's "We Are Yesterday"
bodyHtml:
The blockbuster battle between the Justice League and the Legion of Doom steamrolls into May as parts four and five of the DC All In crossover “We Are Yesterday” hit comic book shops with the May 21 release of Batman/Superman: World’s Finest #39 and Justice League Unlimited #7 on May 28!

The Legion of Doom has the Justice League on the ropes, as “We Are Yesterday” enters parts four and five this May!

Batman/Superman: World’s Finest #39 (Part IV of “We Are Yesterday”)

The fan-favorite storytelling team of writer Mark Waid and artist Dan Mora are putting the pressure on the Dark Knight and the Man of Steel as “We Are Yesterday” enters its third chapter! As Gorilla Grodd’s vicious temporal onslaught upon the Justice League continues, the Batman and Superman from the time of the World’s Finest Super Heroes get a horrifying glimpse into their own futures…and the tragedies to come! This installment of DC’s first DC All In crossover features the first of two connecting covers by artist Mora, with variant covers by Lucio Parrillo, Adrían Gutiérrez, and A.L. Kaplan

.Batman/Superman: World’s Finest #39 On Sale May 21


Justice League Unlimited #7 (Part V of “ We Are Yesterday”)

Waid and artist Travis Moore continue to push the Justice League to the brink in Justice League Unlimited #7, part five of “We Are Yesterday.” The Justice League is fractured throughout time as the Legion of Doom achieves the unthinkable…the siege of the Watchtower! As Gorilla Grodd’s attack intensifies, it’ll be up to one hero to call in the cavalry, and it is not who you think!

Justice League Unlimited #7 On Sale May 28

Destruction, redemption, and a cavalcade of chaos culminate in this penultimate chapter of “We Are Yesterday,” a special crossover with Batman/Superman: World’s Finest! Dan Mora provides the second of two connecting covers, and variant covers provided by Tiago Da Silva, Rahzzah, Yanick Paquette, Travis Moore and Phil Hester, plus an Asian American Pacific Islander Heritage month variant cover by Cliff Chiang, and a “Supersmash-Up” variant by Jorge Molina.

title: Gwen Stacy’s SHOCKING Return: The New Killer Gwenpool Shakes Up Marvel!
bodyHtml:
GWEN STACY RETURNS… AS A KILLER?!

Her death shattered Spider-Man’s world and became one of the most heartbreaking moments in comic book history. But now, she’s back—and in the most shocking way imaginable!

Teased last week, Marvel is bringing Gwen Stacy back from the dead, but not as the girl you remember. This May, New York Times bestselling writer Cavan Scott (Star Wars: The High Republic) and rising star artist Stefano Nesi are unleashing a brand-new Gwenpool series—and this time, Gwen’s got blood on her hands!

That’s right—Gwen Stacy has returned, but she’s darker, deadlier, and completely off the rails as the new Gwenpool! But she’s not the only Gwen in town. The original Gwenpool, Marvel’s fourth-wall-breaking superfan, isn’t about to let someone else hijack her identity. With Spider-Man caught in the middle, these two Gwens are on a collision course that’s bound to turn the Marvel Universe upside down—because as Gwen Poole knows all too well, there’s only room for one Gwenpool!

A DEADLY NEW GWENPOOL WREAKS HAVOC!

A brutal new vigilante is cutting a bloody path through New York City, dealing out justice with extreme prejudice. But who is this rogue Gwenpool? And what does she want with Spider-Man? With guest appearances from Kate Bishop and Jeff the Land Shark, this series is set to shake things up in a big way!

Writer Cavan Scott is beyond excited about bringing Gwen back into the mix:

"What an absolute honor! Getting to shake up both Gwenpool and Spider-Man’s status quo? Sign me up! This isn’t the Gwen you remember—she’s got Weapon X-style abilities, a killer wit, and a mean streak to match. But the big question is: Who brought her back? And what happens when the original Gwenpool realizes her world is being rewritten? Throw in Kate Bishop and Jeff the Land Shark, and you’ve got the kind of chaotic, over-the-top Marvel series I’ve always dreamed of writing!"

For artist Stefano Nesi, this marks his big Marvel Comics debut after turning heads with his work on Batman.

"I couldn’t ask for a better partner than Stefano Nesi!" Scott added. "His gritty, kinetic art—brought to life by Matt Milla’s electrifying colors—makes every fight scene explode off the page. Monsters, mayhem, and murder—it’s all here, and trust me, no one is safe!"

STUNNING COVERS TEASE THE MAYHEM AHEAD!

Marvel’s pulled out all the stops with jaw-dropping cover art from Chad Hardin and Greg Land, giving us a first look at this dangerous new Gwenpool. Meanwhile, Jeehyung Lee delivers a striking take on the classic Gwen Poole, and Paco Medina’s haunting variant—featuring Gwen’s own grave—hints at the dark twists ahead.

The battle of the Gwens kicks off this May—don’t miss it!

heroImage.alt: Gwen Stacy’s SHOCKING Return: The New Killer Gwenpool Shakes Up Marvel!

title: BREAKING: Mr. Terrific: Year One Limited Series Announced at DC!
bodyHtml:
DC Reinvigorates the Origin of One of Its Smartest and Most Resourceful Super Heroes in a New DC All In Limited Series!

This May, the origin of Mr. Terrific is dramatically revisited and expanded in a new DC All In series, Mr. Terrific: Year One!

This six-issue limited series marks the return of Peabody Award-winning journalist, TV writer, and host of the investigative-reporting show Reveal Al Letson to DC, following his 2017 debut in the DC New Talent Showcase. Letson is joined on the series by award-winning artist Valentine De Landro (Black Manta, DC Power: A Celebration, Infinite Frontier, Bitch Planet), colorist Marissa Louise, and letterer Lucas Gattoni. The series will also feature art by Edwin Galmon (Cyborg), who will provide present-day sequences integral to the story.

A genius-level inventor and Olympic champion, Michael Holt’s life was destined for greatness. But that life came to a screeching halt when his wife, Paula, and unborn son were killed in a car accident. The Holts once shared ambitious dreams of using their technology to help others, but after the tragedy, Michael retreats from society, selling Holt Industries to mysterious and craven tech billionaire Athena Prescott. Surrendering his dream sets off an explosive chain of events, taking the exceptionally intelligent Holt from the depths of despair to the heights of heroism as Mr. Terrific.

 “Valentine and I are diving deep and telling the origin story of who Mr. Terrific is as a man, and as a super hero,” said Letson. “I’ve loved Valentine’s work for years, and working on this series has been a dream. I can’t wait for fans to see what we’re cooking up.” 

 “Working with Al, Marissa, Edwin, and Lucas, as well as the DC editorial team, has been an absolute blast,” added artist Valentine De Landro. “I’m excited to contribute to Mr. Terrific’s story, a character I’ve enjoyed reading throughout the years.”

Available for Preorder Friday, February 21

On Sale at Participating Comic Book Shops and Digital Retailers Wednesday, May 28

The debut issue of this six-issue series features a main cover by artist De Landro and series colorist Marissa Louise, with variant covers by Strange Adventures artist Mitch Gerads, Evan “Doc” Shaner, and Denys Cowan (featuring inks and colors by Ho Che Anderson).

Mr. Terrific Year 1 Variant by Denys Cowan & Ho Che Anderson
Mr. Terrific Year 1 Variant by Doc Shaner
Mr. Terrific Year 1 Variant by Mitch Gerads

title: MEGACON 2025: Recap of the Art Adams and John Boy Meyers Sketch Duel
bodyHtml:
One of the most exciting events we attended at the convention was the Sketch Duel between legendary artist Arthur Adams and rising star John Boy Meyers. This unique format brought two talented comic book artists together in a head-to-head drawing challenge, where they created sketches based on audience suggestions within timed rounds. Hosted by Aris Quinones of Variant Comics, the event was filled with engaging conversation, humor, and stunning artwork.

Arthur Adams, a veteran artist known for his work on X-Men, Conan, and Fantastic Four, faced off against John Boy Meyers, an up-and-coming artist recognized for his contributions to Teen Titans, Wonder Woman, and Spawn. The competition consisted of two 15-minute sketching rounds. In the first round, Adams was tasked with drawing a bizarre combination—a Jack Kirby-inspired figure with Spawn’s body and a Hitler mustache—while Meyers sketched Venom. Both delivered impressive and wildly creative pieces that showcased their distinct artistic styles.

During the second round, the artists were given free rein to draw whatever they wanted. While sketching, they answered questions from Quinones about their artistic influences, experiences in the industry, and challenges like creative burnout. Meyers credited Todd McFarlane for mentoring him during his time on Spawn, while Adams shared insights about maintaining longevity in the comic book industry. The mutual admiration between the two artists was evident, as they praised each other’s work and expressed enthusiasm for potential collaborations.

By the end of the round, Meyers had created another incredible Spawn sketch, while Adams surprised everyone with a beautifully detailed Popeye drawing—an unexpected but stunning piece that captivated the audience. Adding to the excitement, the event concluded with a raffle, allowing four lucky fans to take home original artwork from the duel.

The Sketch Duel was a beautiful showcase of artistic talent, camaraderie, and fan engagement. It highlighted not only the impressive skills of Adams and Meyers but also the sense of community that makes comic book conventions so special.

heroImage.alt: MEGACON 2025: Recap of the Art Adams and John Boy Meyers Sketch Duel

title: MEGACON 2025: DC's Absolute Universe Panel Recap
bodyHtml:
We attended the DC’s Absolute Universe panel at MegaCon Orlando 2025, where Scott Snyder led a discussion about the ambitious new line of comics that reimagines classic DC heroes. With strong backing from Jim Lee and DC leadership, Snyder has been instrumental in shaping this bold new universe. The Absolute Batman series has been a massive success, with its first issue selling over half a million copies and subsequent issues continuing to perform exceptionally well. Snyder enthusiastically detailed the future of the Absolute line, including upcoming issues of Absolute Batman, Absolute Superman, and Absolute Wonder Woman.

One of the most exciting announcements involved Absolute Wonder Woman, written by Kelly Thompson with art by Hayden Sherman. This version of Diana is raised by Cersei in hell and wields a magical bag containing an arsenal of weapons. The premise is wildly different yet remains true to Wonder Woman’s core identity. Another major development was Jason Aaron’s involvement in Absolute Superman, initially as a consultant but eventually as the series’ writer. His storytelling approach contrasts sharply with Snyder’s fast-paced style, opting for a slower build-up that promises explosive payoffs in later issues.

The panel also revealed details about the next wave of Absolute titles: Absolute Martian Manhunter, Absolute Green Lantern, and Absolute Flash. Absolute Martian Manhunter, written by Dennis Camp with art by Javier Rodríguez, reimagines J’onn J’onzz as a human FBI agent who merges with a Martian consciousness in a symbiotic relationship reminiscent of Venom. Absolute Green Lantern, penned by Al Ewing with art by rising talent Jahnoy Lindsay, introduces a lone female Green Lantern struggling against a universe where villains hold the upper hand. Meanwhile, Absolute Flash, written by Jeff Lemire with art by Nick Robles, reinvents Wally West without the Speed Force, offering a radically different take on the character.

Another major point of discussion was how these characters would interact within the Absolute Universe. Unlike the traditional DC continuity, where heroes inevitably cross paths, the Absolute line may handle their meetings in unpredictable ways. With a roster of Eisner-winning creators and a fresh perspective on DC’s legendary characters, the Absolute Universe is shaping up to be one of the most exciting developments in modern comics. Fans can look forward to a year of groundbreaking storytelling and stunning artwork as this universe continues to unfold.

heroImage.alt: MEGACON 2025: DC's Absolute Universe Panel Recap

title: DC Comics Unveils 'Justice League Unlimited' with Star-Studded Creative Team
bodyHtml:

The fresh lineup boasts an exciting mix of iconic and dynamic characters, including:

This diverse and powerful team is poised to take on the most daunting challenges in the DC Universe, and with Waid's masterful storytelling and Mora's stunning visuals, fans can expect an unforgettable adventure.

Mark Waid, known for his work on 'Kingdom Come' and 'The Flash', brings his signature blend of action, humor, and heart to the series, while Dan Mora's vibrant art style, showcased in 'Detective Comics' and 'Power Rangers', promises to bring the team to life in a way that will leave readers breathless.

title: DC Absolute Universe Confirmed: Details Inside
bodyHtml:
In an effort to reset their line of comic books WITHOUT rebooting the stories and continuity.

DC Comics has announced the birth of a new Universe in its cosmology. The ABSOLUTE DC UNIVERSE will exist alongside the CLASSIC DC UNIVERSE and both worlds will tell stories that are leading to a larger story down the line.

THIS OCTOBER, DC ALL IN SPECIAL #1 WILL KICK THIS OFF.

"What we came up with, along with a bunch of great creators -- Mark Waid and everybody else working on the lead up to it -- is that, during the events of AP, unbeknownst to the superheroes, Darkseid starts to realize that something has always been missing. He senses something in a special moment during Absolute Power that we don't want to spoil. He senses something has changed, and once he realizes this, it sets him off on a quest that's going to bring him into direct conflict with this new Justice League taht Superman is setting up -- this more robust Justice League. The collision between those two forces will reshape the DC cosmology."

That's what creates "a dangerous, wild new planet" -- the Absolute DC Universe -- where Snyder said they have brought in some of the biggest names in comics as well as some rising stars to kick off an epic story that will drive DC for years to come. If the main universe is "founded on Superman energy," then the Absolute universe is "founded on Darkseid energy." In it, the heroes are underdogs, and the world is darker, so "They have to be tougher, they've got to be more resourceful, they have to shine brighter."

heroImage.alt: DC Absolute Universe Confirmed: Details Inside

title: ALIENS VS AVENGERS CROSSOVER ANNOUNCED BY MARVEL
bodyHtml:
‘ALIENS VS AVENGERS’, a new comic series from writer Jonathan Hickman and artist Esad Ribic.

Releasing on July 24.

Jonathan Hickman spoke to Entertainment Weekly about the series.

"I've never worked on a licensed or 'non-superhero' property the entire time I've been at Marvel, so when all of this came together almost two years ago, I kind of jumped at the opportunity," Hickman tells EW. "I love the Aliens universe and the mythology, and obviously just how atmospherically and well-designed everything is has always gotten my creative juices flowing. It was tricky finding a way to make these two things work together, but I think Esad and I landed on something that works for fans of both franchises.

Probably one of the coolest things about the project is how we've found really fun ways to 'Avengerize' Aliens and 'alien-up' Avengers," Hickman says. "I think fans will be surprised at how elegantly some of those things fit together. It really turned out to be a chocolate-and-peanut-butter situation."

The brief plot synopsis for the crossover says, "It's all led to this: Xenomorphs reach Earth! The perfect organism meets a planet of superhumans. Who will be first to fall?"

heroImage.alt: ALIENS VS AVENGERS CROSSOVER ANNOUNCED BY MARVEL

title: RUMOR: DC Planning Major Refocus. New 52 Done Right?
bodyHtml:
Recent reports from Bleeding Cool suggest that DC Comics is embarking on a bold new initiative called Absolute DC or DC All-In, led by renowned creator Scott Snyder.

Unlike previous creator-focused endeavors like New 52 or DC Rebirth, DC All-In takes a different approach, aiming for a hands-off strategy that grants creators significant creative freedom. Mark Waid's Summer event is being called Absolute Power. It’s reasonable to believe this new initiative will launch on the other side of this event.

Let’s delves into the details of this groundbreaking initiative and its implications for the future of DC Comics.

The Vision Behind DC All-In:

Rather than following a single central creator-focused line or a major editorial driver, DC All-In seeks to empower creators with a carte blanche approach reminiscent of the All-Star line. Snyder and his team envision an initiative that spotlights DC's entire lineup while fostering innovation and creativity. This hands-off approach is a departure from previous reboot or relaunch strategies, as DC All-In aims to build upon existing continuity rather than starting anew.

Integration with the Main Line:

One notable aspect of DC All-In is its integration with the main DC Comics lineup. Unlike standalone projects like All-Star or the Ultimate Universe, DC All-In publications will be released in conjunction with the main line, forming part of a larger initiative to showcase the breadth and depth of DC's universe. This integration ensures that fans can enjoy a cohesive reading experience while exploring new and exciting stories.

A Holistic Approach to Rejuvenation:

DC All-In is seen as a deliberate effort to learn from past successes and failures, particularly with initiatives like New 52 and DC Rebirth. Rather than simply rebooting or relaunching the universe, Snyder and his team are taking a more holistic approach, aiming to rejuvenate the DC Comics brand while preserving what already works. This approach reflects a commitment to both honoring the rich history of DC Comics and pushing the boundaries of storytelling.

As details about DC All-In continue to emerge, anticipation is building for its official unveiling at San Diego Comic-Con. Fans can expect to learn more about the creative teams involved, the titles slated for release, and the overarching vision for the initiative. With promises of bold new stories and a fresh perspective on the DC Universe, DC All-In is poised to make a significant impact on the comic book industry.

DC All-In represents a bold new direction for DC Comics, one that prioritizes creative freedom, integration with the main line, and a holistic approach to rejuvenation. Led by Scott Snyder and a team of A-list creators, this initiative promises to deliver bold new stories while honoring the legacy of DC Comics. As the comic book world eagerly awaits further details, one thing is clear: DC All-In is poised to redefine the future of superhero storytelling.

heroImage.alt: RUMOR: DC Planning Major Refocus. New 52 Done Right?

title: "Marvel Offers Free Access to Iconic Comics Ahead of 'FANTASTIC FOUR' Film Release"
bodyHtml:
Marvel Studios has recently announced an exciting initiative to offer free access to five iconic comics that have inspired the highly anticipated upcoming film, 'FANTASTIC FOUR'. This move not only serves as a treat for fans eagerly awaiting the movie's release but also provides an opportunity to delve into the rich history and source material that have shaped the beloved superhero team.

Among the selection of comics made available for free are some of the most pivotal issues in the Fantastic Four's storied legacy. First and foremost is 'Fantastic Four #1', originally published in 1961, which introduced readers to the groundbreaking superhero team created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. This landmark issue not only laid the foundation for the Marvel Universe as we know it but also set the stage for countless adventures to come.

Additionally, Marvel is offering access to 'Fantastic Four #48 - 50', a storyline often hailed as one of the greatest in comic book history. These issues mark the first appearance of two iconic characters, Galactus and the Silver Surfer, whose cosmic presence has left an indelible mark on the Marvel mythos. Their introduction expanded the scope of the Fantastic Four's adventures into the far reaches of space, introducing themes of cosmic exploration and existential threats that would become synonymous with the franchise.

Rounding out the selection is 'Fantastic Four: Life Story #1', a more recent addition to the Fantastic Four canon published in 2021. This series reimagines the team's journey through the decades, offering a fresh perspective on their enduring legacy and exploring how their exploits might unfold in a changing world.

By offering free access to these seminal comics, Marvel aims to celebrate the rich history and enduring appeal of the Fantastic Four while inviting both longtime fans and newcomers alike to experience the magic of Marvel storytelling. As excitement builds for the upcoming film adaptation, now is the perfect time to dive into the pages of these timeless classics and witness the fantastic adventures that have captivated readers for generations.

heroImage.alt: "Marvel Offers Free Access to Iconic Comics Ahead of 'FANTASTIC FOUR' Film Release"

title: Jason Todd Lives! ‘DEATH IN THE FAMILY: ROBIN LIVES!’ Announced
bodyHtml:
"In 1988, DC raised the revolutionary idea to let fans decide the fate of then-Robin Jason Todd by calling a 1-800 number and casting a vote," reads DC's official announcement. "Originally, fans decided that Jason would pay the ultimate price at the hands of The Joker, but a new facsimile of the original Batman #428 was released in December of 2023, featuring an alternate ending in which Jason lives. Now, for the first time, readers will witness his plans for revenge against the Clown Prince of Crime."

In a groundbreaking move, DC Comics announced the launch of a 4-issue comic miniseries titled 'Death in the Family: Robin Lives!'

Hellboy's Mike Mignola returns to do a variant cover for issue 1. Mignola originally did the covers for Batman: Death in the Family.

The miniseries, set to debut in July, promises to explore an alternate reality where Robin, the beloved sidekick of the Dark Knight, escapes the clutches of death at the hands of the infamous Joker.

Written by the acclaimed J.M. DeMatteis and brought to life through the stunning artwork of Rick Leonardi, 'Death in the Family: Robin Lives!' offers a fresh perspective on one of the most iconic moments in comic book history.

Instead of succumbing to the tragic fate that befell him in the original storyline, Robin embarks on a new journey filled with twists, turns, and unexpected challenges.

Freed from the constraints of his tragic demise, Robin grapples with a newfound sense of purpose and identity as he navigates the perilous streets of Gotham City.

Prepare to witness the ultimate resurrection as Robin defies fate and embarks on a quest for redemption in 'Death in the Family: Robin Lives!' This is not just a comic miniseries; it's a bold reimagining of one of the most iconic stories in comic book history—a testament to the enduring power of hope, heroism, and the undying spirit of the Boy Wonder.
